title: /kernel
summary_text: `/kernel` launch stack in this repo checks these entrypoints (in order of call graph): - `scripts/local/launchers/kernel` ŌåÆ tries: - `k latest` / `k open` - non-interactive: `k new ...` via `KERNEL_K_NO_ATTACH=true` - fallback: `codex-kernel-guard launch ...` - final fallback: `codex-prompt-launch kernel ...` - `scripts/local/kernel-entrypoint.sh` (4-pane path) expects: - `FOUR_PANE_LAUNCH_SCRIPT` (`scripts/local/kernel-4pane-launch.sh`) - `PROMPT_LAUNCH_BIN` defaulting to `$HOME/bin/codex-prompt-launch` - `scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh launch` requires launch shape validation and then executes `scripts/lib/kernel-codex-thread.sh launch` - `scripts/lib/kernel-codex-thread.sh launch` requires interactive TTY and a resolvable `codex` binary. PATH / wrapper expectations inferred: - `k` (from `scripts/local/launchers/kernel`) for open/new in launcher branch; optional if that branch is skipped. - `codex-prompt-launch` (or `KERNEL_CODEX_PROMPT_LAUNCH_BIN`) for hard fallback if no 4-pane/kernel guard route is used. - `codex` (or `CODEX_BIN`, or `$HOME/bin/codex`, `$HOME/.local/bin/codex`, `/usr/local/bin/codex`, `/opt/homebrew/bin/codex`) is required for actual thread execution. - `gemini` / `cursor` / `copilot` / `gh` for specialist provider availability checks (`copilot` path can also be `gh`). - `jq`, `bash`, `python3` are used by scripts. - `tmux` is used by launcher 4-pane and thread/session checks when present. `kernel-root` behavior: - `scripts/local/launchers/kernel` expects `kernel-root` relative to launcher dir, and `scripts/local/launchers/k4` shows a similar pattern with fallback to `$HOME/bin/kernel-root`. - In this repo there is no `scripts/local/launchers/kernel-root` file, so that local fallback path is effectively unavailable unless externally installed. Whether missing launchers should block bootstrap: - Missing `k` does **not** block bootstrap; the code falls back through next stage. - Missing `codex-prompt-launch` is a **hard stop** if reached (explicit `[ -x "${PROMPT_LAUNCH_BIN}" ] || exit 1`). - Missing `codex` is effectively a hard stop at execution time in `kernel-codex-thread.sh launch` (it exits with ŌĆ£codex executable not foundŌĆØ). - Missing specialist wrappers/credentials can block bootstrap earlier in `codex-kernel-guard.sh` via `launch_validate_shape`/`glm_credentials_present` (requires lane shape validity).
